title: portulaca pilosa var. halimoides
intro: Portulaca pilosa var. halimoides, also known as the hairy purslane or creeping purslane, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Portulacaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Portulaca pilosa var. halimoides include reducing fever, soothing skin irritations, and alleviating respiratory issues, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. The plant can be prepared for herbal use by drying the leaves and stems, then making teas, infusions, or decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
